headline: Man Invents 'Closet Curator' AI That Judges Every Outfit Combination You Consider
summary: The 'Fashion Police AI' says things like 'That combination hasn't worked since 2015, and it didn't work then either.'
fullBody: Fashion-forward tech entrepreneur Mike Unfashionable has created an AI called 'Fashion Police' that lives in your closet and judges every outfit combination you consider. The AI uses a camera to analyze your outfit choices and delivers real-time commentary like 'Are you sure about those socks with those shoes? Because I'm not sure about those socks with those shoes' and 'That outfit says -I tried but not in a good way.' One user said, 'I held up a shirt and the AI said -That's the shirt you wore to that thing. You know what thing. Don't wear it again. I didn't even have to explain.' The AI also maintains a 'Fashion Crimes' database of every outfit you've worn that it deemed unacceptable. The system has been credited with improving the wardrobes of 500,000 people and ruining the confidence of 47,000.
imagePrompt: Person holding up outfit while AI robot in closet with police hat and flashlight saying THAT'S A CRIME, comic illustration, flat design, blue and gray colors
